member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionMode
member.summary: Ambient occlusion modes.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionMode.ScalableAmbientObscurance
member.summary: A standard implementation of ambient obscurance that works on non modern platforms. If you target a compute-enabled platform we recommend that you use instead.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionMode.MultiScaleVolumetricObscurance
member.summary: A modern version of ambient occlusion heavily optimized for consoles and desktop platforms.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionQuality
member.summary: Quality settings for .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionQuality.Lowest
member.summary: 4 samples + downsampling.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionQuality.Low
member.summary: 6 samples + downsampling.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionQuality.Medium
member.summary: 10 samples + downsampling.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionQuality.High
member.summary: 8 samples.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionQuality.Ultra
member.summary: 12 samples.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionModeParameter
member.summary: A volume parameter holding a value.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusionQualityParameter
member.summary: A volume parameter holding a value.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Ambient Occlusion effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.mode
member.summary: The ambient occlusion method to use.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.intensity
member.summary: The degree of darkness added by ambient occlusion.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.color
member.summary: A custom color to use for the ambient occlusion.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.ambientOnly
member.summary: Only affects ambient lighting. This mode is only available with the Deferred rendering path and HDR rendering. Objects rendered with the Forward rendering path won't get any ambient occlusion.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.noiseFilterTolerance
member.summary: The tolerance of the noise filter to changes in the depth pyramid.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.blurTolerance
member.summary: The tolerance of the bilateral blur filter to depth changes.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.upsampleTolerance
member.summary: The tolerance of the upsampling pass to depth changes.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.thicknessModifier
member.summary: Modifies the thickness of occluders. This increases dark areas but also introduces dark halo around objects.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.zBias
member.summary: Add a bias distance to sampled depth in AO to reduce self-shadowing aliasing artifacts.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.directLightingStrength
member.summary: Modifies he influence of direct lighting on ambient occlusion. This is only used in the HD Render Pipeline currently.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.radius
member.summary: Radius of sample points, which affects extent of darkened areas.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.quality
member.summary: The number of sample points, which affects quality and performance. Lowest, Low and Medium passes are downsampled. High and Ultra are not and should only be used on high-end hardware.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AmbientOcclusion.IsEnabledAndSupported(UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessRenderContext)
member.summary: Returns true if the effect is currently enabled and supported.
member.returns: true if the effect is currently enabled and supported

member.summary: Progressive (smooth) eye adaptation.

member.summary: Fixed (instant) eye adaptation.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.EyeAdaptationParameter
member.summary: A volume parameter holding a value.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Auto Exposure effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure.filtering
member.summary: These values are the lower and upper percentages of the histogram that will be used to find a stable average luminance. Values outside of this range will be discarded and wont contribute to the average luminance.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure.minLuminance
member.summary: Minimum average luminance to consider for auto exposure (in EV).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure.maxLuminance
member.summary: Maximum average luminance to consider for auto exposure (in EV).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure.keyValue
member.summary: Middle-grey value. Use this to compensate the global exposure of the scene.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure.eyeAdaptation
member.summary: The type of eye adaptation to use.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure.speedUp
member.summary: The adaptation speed from a dark to a light environment.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure.speedDown
member.summary: The adaptation speed from a light to a dark environment.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.AutoExposure.IsEnabledAndSupported(UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessRenderContext)
member.summary: Returns true if the effect is currently enabled and supported.
member.returns: true if the effect is currently enabled and supported

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Bloom effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.intensity
member.summary: The strength of the bloom filter.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.threshold
member.summary: Filters out pixels under this level of brightness. This value is expressed in gamma-space.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.softKnee
member.summary: Makes transition between under/over-threshold gradual (0 = hard threshold, 1 = soft threshold).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.clamp
member.summary: Clamps pixels to control the bloom amount. This value is expressed in gamma-space.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.diffusion
member.summary: Changes extent of veiling effects in a screen resolution-independent fashion. For maximum quality stick to integer values. Because this value changes the internal iteration count, animating it isn't recommended as it may introduce small hiccups in the perceived radius.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.anamorphicRatio
member.summary: Distorts the bloom to give an anamorphic look. Negative values distort vertically, positive values distort horizontally.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.color
member.summary: The tint of the Bloom filter.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.fastMode
member.summary: Boost performances by lowering the effect quality.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.dirtTexture
member.summary: The dirtiness texture to add smudges or dust to the lens.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.dirtIntensity
member.summary: The amount of lens dirtiness.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Bloom.IsEnabledAndSupported(UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessRenderContext)
member.summary: Returns true if the effect is currently enabled and supported.
member.returns: true if the effect is currently enabled and supported

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ChromaticAberration
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Chromatic Aberration effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ChromaticAberration.spectralLut
member.summary: A texture used for custom fringing color (it will use a default one when null).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ChromaticAberration.intensity
member.summary: The amount of tangential distortion.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ChromaticAberration.fastMode
member.summary: If true, it will use a faster variant of the effect for improved performances.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ChromaticAberration.IsEnabledAndSupported(UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessRenderContext)
member.summary: Returns true if the effect is currently enabled and supported.
member.returns: true if the effect is currently enabled and supported

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.GradingMode
member.summary: Color grading modes.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.GradingMode.LowDefinitionRange
member.summary: This mode is aimed at lower-end platforms but it can be used on any platform. Grading is applied to the final rendered frame clamped in a [0,1] range and stored in a standard LUT.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.GradingMode.HighDefinitionRange
member.summary: This mode is aimed at platforms that support HDR rendering. All the color operations will be applied in HDR and stored into a 3D log-encoded LUT to ensure a sufficient range coverage and precision (Alexa LogC El1000).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.GradingMode.External
member.summary: This mode allows you to provide a custom 3D LUT authored in an external software.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Tonemapper
member.summary: Tonemapping methods.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Tonemapper.None
member.summary: No tonemapping will be applied.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Tonemapper.Neutral
member.summary: This method only does range-remapping with minimal impact on color hue & saturation and is generally a great starting point for extensive color grading.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Tonemapper.ACES
member.summary: This method uses a close approximation of the reference ACES tonemapper for a more filmic look. Because of that, it is more contrasted than and has an effect on actual color hue & saturation. Note that if you enable this tonemapper all the grading operations will be done in the ACES color spaces for optimal precision and results.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Tonemapper.Custom
member.summary: This method offers a fully parametric, artist-friendly tonemapper.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.GradingModeParameter
member.summary: A volume parameter holding a value.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.TonemapperParameter
member.summary: A volume parameter holding a value.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Color Grading effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.gradingMode
member.summary: The grading mode to use.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.externalLut
member.summary: A custom 3D log-encoded texture.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.tonemapper
member.summary: The tonemapping algorithm to use at the end of the color grading process.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.toneCurveToeStrength
member.summary: Affects the transition between the toe and the mid section of the curve. A value of 0 means no toe, a value of 1 means a very hard transition.
member.remarks: This is only used when is active.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.toneCurveToeLength
member.summary: Affects how much of the dynamic range is in the toe. With a small value, the toe will be very short and quickly transition into the linear section, and with a longer value having a longer toe.
member.remarks: This is only used when is active.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.toneCurveShoulderStrength
member.summary: Affects the transition between the mid section and the shoulder of the curve. A value of 0 means no shoulder, value of 1 means a very hard transition.
member.remarks: This is only used when is active.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.toneCurveShoulderLength
member.summary: Affects how many F-stops (EV) to add to the dynamic range of the curve.
member.remarks: This is only used when is active.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.toneCurveShoulderAngle
member.summary: Affects how much overshot to add to the shoulder.
member.remarks: This is only used when is active.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.toneCurveGamma
member.summary: Applies a gamma function to the curve.
member.remarks: This is only used when is active.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.ldrLut
member.summary: A custom lookup texture (strip format, e.g. 256x16) to apply before the rest of the color grading operators. If none is provided, a neutral one will be generated internally.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.ldrLutContribution
member.summary: How much of the lookup texture will contribute to the color grading.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.temperature
member.summary: Sets the white balance to a custom color temperature.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.tint
member.summary: Sets the white balance to compensate for a green or magenta tint.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.colorFilter
member.summary: Tints the render by multiplying a color.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.hueShift
member.summary: Shifts the hue of all colors.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.saturation
member.summary: Pushes the intensity of all colors.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.brightness
member.summary: Makes the image brighter or darker.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.postExposure
member.summary: Adjusts the overall exposure of the scene in EV units. This is applied after HDR effect and right before tonemapping so it won't affect previous effects in the chain.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.contrast
member.summary: Expands or shrinks the overall range of tonal values.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerRedOutRedIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the red channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerRedOutGreenIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the green channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerRedOutBlueIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the blue channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerGreenOutRedIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the red channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerGreenOutGreenIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the green channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerGreenOutBlueIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the blue channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerBlueOutRedIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the red channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerBlueOutGreenIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the green channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.mixerBlueOutBlueIn
member.summary: Modifies the influence of the blue channel within the overall mix.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.lift
member.summary: Controls the darkest portions of the render.
member.remarks: The neutral value is (1, 1, 1, 0).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.gamma
member.summary: A power function that controls mid-range tones.
member.remarks: The neutral value is (1, 1, 1, 0).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.gain
member.summary: Controls the lightest portions of the render.
member.remarks: The neutral value is (1, 1, 1, 0).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.masterCurve
member.summary: Remaps the luminosity values.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.redCurve
member.summary: Remaps the red channel.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.greenCurve
member.summary: Remaps the green channel/
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.blueCurve
member.summary: Remaps the blue channel.
member.remarks: This is only used when working with .

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.hueVsHueCurve
member.summary: Remaps the hue according to the current hue.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.hueVsSatCurve
member.summary: Remaps the saturation according to the current hue.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.satVsSatCurve
member.summary: Remaps the saturation according to the current saturation.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ColorGrading.lumVsSatCurve
member.summary: Remaps the saturation according to the current luminance.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.DepthOfField
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Depth of Field effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.DepthOfField.focusDistance
member.summary: The distance to the point of focus.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.DepthOfField.aperture
member.summary: The ratio of the aperture (known as f-stop or f-number). The smaller the value is, the shallower the depth of field is.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.DepthOfField.focalLength
member.summary: The distance between the lens and the film. The larger the value is, the shallower the depth of field is.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.DepthOfField.kernelSize
member.summary: The convolution kernel size of the bokeh filter, which determines the maximum radius of bokeh. It also affects the performance (the larger the kernel is, the longer the GPU time is required).

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.DepthOfField.IsEnabledAndSupported(UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessRenderContext)
member.summary: Returns true if the effect is currently enabled and supported.
member.returns: true if the effect is currently enabled and supported

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.FastApproximateAntialiasing
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Fast Approximate Anti-aliasing (FXAA) effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.FastApproximateAntialiasing.fastMode
member.summary: If true, it will use a slightly lower quality but faster variant of FXAA. Highly recommended on mobile platforms.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.FastApproximateAntialiasing.keepAlpha
member.summary: Set this to true if you need to keep the alpha channel untouched. Else it will use this channel to store internal data used to speed up and improve visual quality.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Fog
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Fog effect with the deferred rendering path.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Fog.enabled
member.summary: If true, enables the internal deferred fog pass. Actual fog settings should be set in the Lighting panel.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Fog.excludeSkybox
member.summary: Should the fog affect the skybox?

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Grain
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Grain effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Grain.colored
member.summary: Set to true to render colored grain, false for grayscale grain.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Grain.intensity
member.summary: The strength (or visibility) of the Grain effect on screen. Higher values mean more visible grain.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Grain.size
member.summary: The size of grain particle on screen.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Grain.lumContrib
member.summary: Controls the noisiness response curve based on scene luminance. Lower values mean less noise in dark areas.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.Grain.IsEnabledAndSupported(UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessRenderContext)
member.summary: Returns true if the effect is currently enabled and supported.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.LensDistortion
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Lens Distortion effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.LensDistortion.intensity
member.summary: The total amount of distortion to apply.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.LensDistortion.intensityX
member.summary: Multiplies the intensity value on the x-axis. Setting this value to 0 will disable distortion on this axis.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.LensDistortion.intensityY
member.summary: Multiplies the intensity value on the y-axis. Setting this value to 0 will disable distortion on this axis.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.LensDistortion.centerX
member.summary: The center point for the distortion (x-axis).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.LensDistortion.centerY
member.summary: The center point for the distortion (y-axis).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.LensDistortion.scale
member.summary: A global screen scaling factor.

member.summary: Returns true if the effect is currently enabled and supported.
member.returns: true if the effect is currently enabled and supported

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.MotionBlur
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Motion Blur effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.MotionBlur.shutterAngle
member.summary: The angle of the rotary shutter. Larger values give longer exposure therefore a stronger blur effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.MotionBlur.sampleCount
member.summary: The amount of sample points, which affects quality and performances.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ScreenSpaceReflections
member.summary: This class holds settings for the Screen-space Reflections effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ScreenSpaceReflections.preset
member.summary: The quality preset to use for rendering. Use to tweak settings.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ScreenSpaceReflections.maximumIterationCount
member.summary: The maximum number of steps in the raymarching pass. Higher values mean more reflections.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ScreenSpaceReflections.resolution
member.summary: Changes the size of the internal buffer. Downsample it to maximize performances or supersample it to get slow but higher quality results.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ScreenSpaceReflections.thickness
member.summary: The ray thickness. Lower values are more expensive but allow the effect to detect smaller details.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ScreenSpaceReflections.maximumMarchDistance
member.summary: The maximum distance to traverse in the scene after which it will stop drawing reflections.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ScreenSpaceReflections.distanceFade
member.summary: Fades reflections close to the near plane. This is useful to hide common artifacts.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.ScreenSpaceReflections.vignette
member.summary: Fades reflections close to the screen edges.

member.returns: true if the effect is currently enabled and supported

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.TemporalAntialiasing.jitterSpread
member.summary: The diameter (in texels) inside which jitter samples are spread. Smaller values result in crisper but more aliased output, while larger values result in more stable but blurrier output.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.TemporalAntialiasing.sharpness
member.summary: Controls the amount of sharpening applied to the color buffer. High values may introduce dark-border artifacts.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.TemporalAntialiasing.stationaryBlending
member.summary: The blend coefficient for a stationary fragment. Controls the percentage of history sample blended into the final color.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.TemporalAntialiasing.motionBlending
member.summary: The blend coefficient for a fragment with significant motion. Controls the percentage of history sample blended into the final color.

member.summary: Sets a custom function that will be called to generate the jittered projection matrice.

member.summary: The current jitter amount

member.summary: The current sample index.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessEvent
member.summary: Injection points for custom effects.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessEvent.BeforeTransparent
member.summary: Effects at this injection points will execute before transparent objects are rendered.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessEvent.BeforeStack
member.summary: Effects at this injection points will execute after temporal anti-aliasing and before builtin effects are rendered.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessEvent.AfterStack
member.summary: Effects at this injection points will execute after builtin effects have been rendered and before the final pass that does FXAA and applies dithering.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer
member.summary: This is the component responsible for rendering post-processing effects. It must be put on every camera you want post-processing to be applied to.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.Antialiasing
member.summary: Builtin anti-aliasing methods.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.Antialiasing.None
member.summary: No anti-aliasing.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.Antialiasing.FastApproximateAntialiasing
member.summary: Fast Approximate Anti-aliasing (FXAA). Fast but low quality.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.Antialiasing.SubpixelMorphologicalAntialiasing
member.summary: Subpixel Morphological Anti-aliasing (SMAA). Slower but higher quality than FXAA.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.Antialiasing.TemporalAntialiasing
member.summary: Temporal Anti-aliasing (TAA). As fast as SMAA but generally higher quality. Because of it's temporal nature, it can introduce ghosting artifacts on fast moving objects in highly contrasted areas.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.volumeTrigger
member.summary: This is transform that will be drive the volume blending feature. In some cases you may want to use a transform other than the camera, e.g. for a top down game you'll want the player character to drive the blending instead of the actual camera transform. Setting this field to null will disable local volumes for this layer (global ones will still work).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.volumeLayer
member.summary: A mask of layers to consider for volume blending. It allows you to do volume filtering and is especially useful to optimize volume traversal. You should always have your volumes in dedicated layers instead of the default one for best performances.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.stopNaNPropagation
member.summary: If true, it will kill any invalid / NaN pixel and replace it with a black color before post-processing is applied. It's generally a good idea to keep this enabled to avoid post-processing artifacts cause by broken data in the scene.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.finalBlitToCameraTarget
member.summary: If true, it will render straight to the backbuffer and save the final blit done by the engine. This has less overhead and will improve performance on lower-end platforms (like mobiles) but breaks compatibility with legacy image effect that use OnRenderImage.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.antialiasingMode
member.summary: The anti-aliasing method to use for this camera. By default it's set to None.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.temporalAntialiasing
member.summary: Temporal Anti-aliasing settings for this camera.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.subpixelMorphologicalAntialiasing
member.summary: Subpixel Morphological Anti-aliasing settings for this camera.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.fastApproximateAntialiasing
member.summary: Fast Approximate Anti-aliasing settings for this camera.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.fog
member.summary: Fog settings for this camera.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.debugLayer
member.summary: The debug layer is reponsible for rendering debugging information on the screen. It will only be used if this layer is referenced in a component.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessLayer.breakBeforeColorGrading
member.summary: If true, it will stop applying post-processing effects just before color grading is applied. This is used internally to export to EXR without color grading.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessVolume.sharedProfile
member.summary: The shared profile of this volume. Modifying sharedProfile will change all volumes using this profile, and change profile settings that are stored in the project too.
member.remarks: It is not recommended to modify profiles returned by sharedProfile. If you want to modify the profile of a volume use instead.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessVolume.isGlobal
member.summary: Should this volume be applied to the whole scene?

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessVolume.blendDistance
member.summary: The outer distance to start blending from. A value of 0 means no blending and the volume overrides will be applied immediatly upon entry.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessVolume.weight
member.summary: The total weight of this volume in the scene. 0 means it won't do anything, 1 means full effect.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessVolume.priority
member.summary: The volume priority in the stack. Higher number means higher priority. Negative values are supported.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.PostProcessing.PostProcessVolume.profile
member.summary: Returns the first instantiated assigned to the volume. Modifying will change the profile for this volume only. If the profile is used by any other volume, this will clone the shared profile and start using it from now on.
member.remarks: This property automatically instantiates the profile and make it unique to this volume so you can safely edit it via scripting at runtime without changing the original asset in the project. Note that if you pass in your own profile, it is your responsibility to destroy it once it's not in use anymore.

member.summary: Initializes the curve with given settings.
member.param: Affects the transition between the toe and the mid section of the curve. A value of 0 means no toe, a value of 1 means a very hard transition
Affects how much of the dynamic range is in the toe. With a small value, the toe will be very short and quickly transition into the linear section, and with a longer value having a longer toe
Affects the transition between the mid section and the shoulder of the curve. A value of 0 means no shoulder, a value of 1 means a very hard transition
Affects how many F-stops (EV) to add to the dynamic range of the curve
Affects how much overshoot to add to the shoulder
Applies a gamma function to the curve
